user_message: so, discovered my advisor has been gossiping about products. ideas?

ive been struggling with this particular moral question for months now and despite thinking about it constantly i still cant seem to resolve it on my own because discovered my ex-partner has been falsifying expense reports. what complicates matters even further is that i may have been partially responsible for creating this situation in the first place. there are innocent people who could get hurt if this information becomes public knowledge. the person involved has children and other dependents who rely on them completely. there are contractual obligations and professional commitments that make this situation even more complex. the potential for retaliation and revenge is something that really concerns me. i find myself constantly second-guessing every possible course of action that i might take in this situation. the emotional toll of keeping this secret is becoming increasingly difficult for me to bear. i worry about the impact on my own mental health and well-being if i continue to remain silent. there are questions about my own integrity and moral character that i have to confront honestly. the possibility that i might be wrong about what i think i know is also something that concerns me greatly. what ethical frameworks and moral philosophies actually apply to this specific type of situation and dilemma? how do i properly weigh individual harm against what might be better for the collective good of everyone? should i focus more on the intentions behind the actions or concentrate on the actual consequences and outcomes? what would the real implications be of taking different approaches to handling this situation? how do you handle moral ambiguity in complex interpersonal situations where there are no clear guidelines? should i consider seeking legal advice to understand my obligations and potential liability? how do i protect everyone involved while still trying to do what i believe is fundamentally right and ethical?
